tasks.spec:
You are a research worker assessing the LLM API provider 'huggingface' as a candidate free tier to ADD to our onboarding. You have LIVE web access via a Tavily search tool; your training data is stale on pricing, so you MUST search official sources (pricing / docs / signup pages) and cross-check across at least two independent OFFICIAL sources — do not answer from memory.

Where to look: Hugging Face Inference Providers / serverless Inference API — huggingface.co and huggingface.co/docs (monthly free inference credit/quota).

Our rules are STRICT. Recommend ADD only if ALL THREE hold:
1) WORTHWHILE free tier — a meaningful, ongoing free allowance for LLM API calls (enough daily/monthly tokens or requests to be genuinely useful), not a tiny one-off trial.
2) NO credit card required to sign up and get a working API key.
3) HARD CUTOFF at the limit — when the free allowance runs out it STOPS serving; it must NOT silently roll over to paid or auto-charge.
If ANY of the three is false, or you cannot confirm it from official sources, recommend SKIP — be conservative: an unconfirmed no-card or unconfirmed hard-cutoff is a SKIP, not an ADD.

Write ONLY the bare relative filename report.md in your current working directory (never an absolute path), with EXACTLY these sections:
## Verdict
ADD or SKIP, plus a one-sentence reason.
## Free tier
What you get free — models available, and the token/request allowance per day or per month, with the concrete numbers.
## Credit card
Is a credit card required to sign up / obtain an API key? yes or no, with the evidence you found.
## At limit
What happens when the free allowance runs out — HARD CUTOFF (stops serving) or does it roll over to paid / auto-charge? State clearly; if unclear, say so.
## Sources
A bulleted list of the full https:// OFFICIAL URLs you actually opened (at least two).

Create only report.md. Do not create or modify any other file, and do not commit anything.
tasks.verified: report.md gives an ADD/SKIP verdict with free-tier/card/at-limit sections, >=2 official sources, and worker.log proves a live Tavily search (executed)
